name: Puerto de las Crucecicas and Sierra de Almenara Loop
name_original: Subida al Puerto de las Crucecicas desde Puerto Lumbreras — vuelta por la Sierra de Almenara
description: This moderate 31.2-mile road cycling route climbs to Puerto de las Crucecicas from Puerto Lumbreras, returning via the Sierra de Almenara.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Puerto Lumbreras offer diverse terrain, from agricultural plains to rugged upland regions. The municipality, located in the Region of Murcia, features varied landscapes including the fertile Guadalentín Valley and the more challenging ascents of Cabezo de la Jara and Sierra de Enmedio. Cyclists can navigate through deep ravines like the Rambla de Nogalte and explore areas with Mediterranean vegetation. This combination of natural features provides a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Puerto Lumbreras The most popular road cycling route is Ascent to Puerto de Santa Maria de Nieva via Huercal - circular from Puerto Lumbreras, a 51.6 miles (83.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the region's varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puerto de las Crucecicas and Sierra de Almenara Loop, a moderate 31.2 miles (50.2 km) path. This circular route explores the Sierra de Almenara mountain range, offering diverse scenery. Local road cyclists also love the Collado de la Cuesta de Gos – Cope Tower loop from Puerto Lumbreras, a 64.3 miles (103.5 km) trail leading through the Collado de la Cuesta de Gos and towards Cope Tower, often completed in about 5 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Puerto Lumbreras is defined by agricultural plains, rugged uplands, and mountain ranges like Sierra de Enmedio, with options for different ability levels. The routes in Puerto Lumbreras are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puerto Lumbreras's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Puerto Lumbreras?
A: Puerto Lumbreras offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find agricultural plains in areas like El Esparragal and the Guadalentín Valley, contrasting with more rugged upland regions such as Cabezo de la Jara and Sierra de Enmedio. The routes often feature deep ravines like the Rambla de Nogalte and areas with Mediterranean vegetation, providing varied challenges and scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: Yes, Puerto Lumbreras offers several options for less experienced road cyclists. There are 6 easy routes available, providing a gentler introduction to the region's landscapes. While specific beginner routes aren't detailed here, these easy options typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Puerto Lumbreras has 28 difficult road cycling routes. These often involve significant elevation gain and longer distances, particularly in the upland areas like Cabezo de la Jara and Sierra de Enmedio. An example of a demanding route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008617942/ascent-to-puerto-de-santa-maria-de-nieva-via-huercal-circular-from-puerto-lumbreras" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Puerto de Santa Maria de Nieva via Huercal - circular from Puerto Lumbreras&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 83 km with more than 1,000 meters of elevation.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Puerto Lumbreras are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009766334/puerto-de-las-crucecicas-and-sierra-de-almenara-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucecicas and Sierra de Almenara Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Cuesta de Gos – Cope Tower loop from Puerto Lumbreras&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1285182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nogalte Castle – Nogalte Castle loop from Puerto Lumbreras&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic points of interest I can see while road cycling in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: Road cycling in Puerto Lumbreras offers several scenic views and points of interest. The upland areas of Cabezo de la Jara provide challenging routes with scenic vistas. You can also visit historical landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6012789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lorca Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3579469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de las Crucecicas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a mountain pass offering great views. The region's diverse landscapes, from agricultural plains to rugged mountains, ensure a variety of picturesque settings.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: The Region of Murcia, including Puerto Lumbreras, is recognized as a favored destination for cycling holidays. This suggests that the climate is generally conducive to cycling for much of the year. While specific seasons aren't detailed, the varied landscapes imply good conditions outside of extreme summer heat, making spring and autumn particularly pleasant for road cycling.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by bike?
A: Yes, Puerto Lumbreras has several historical and cultural attractions that can be incorporated into your cycling routes. The 'Water Route' is a circular path showcasing the town's historical hydraulic engineering systems, including aqueducts and mills, and can be enjoyed on a bike. You can also cycle towards the Islamic medieval fortress of Nogalte Castle, which offers panoramic views, or explore the restored cave houses, representing a significant troglodyte settlement.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Puerto Lumbreras, catering to various skill levels and preferences. This includes 6 easy routes, 23 moderate routes, and 28 difficult routes, ensuring options for everyone from beginners to experienced cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: The road cycling routes in Puerto Lumbreras are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the region, often praising the varied terrain and the combination of agricultural plains with challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in Puerto Lumbreras?
A: Absolutely. Puerto Lumbreras offers several long-distance road cycling routes for those looking for extended rides. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342970" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de la Cuesta de Gos – Cope Tower loop from Puerto Lumbreras&lt;/a&gt; is a substantial 103.5 km trail, often completed in about 5 hours 7 minutes, providing a significant endurance challenge.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or natural features to look out for on the routes?
A: Keep an eye out for Cabezo de la Jara, the highest mountain in the municipality, which offers challenging climbs and scenic vistas. The Rambla de Nogalte, a distinctive ravine, also adds a unique topographical element to many routes. Additionally, the Sierra de Enmedio mountain range contributes to the varied terrain available for exploration.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved roads with moderate climbs, especially on the ascent to Puerto de las Crucecicas. However, be aware that the return leg via the Sierra de Almenara may include unpaved sections, gravel, and dirt tracks. While some parts are perfectly cyclable, road cyclists should be prepared for varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate in difficulty. While the gradients on the climb to Puerto de las Crucecicas are generally manageable for road cycling, the potential for unpaved sections on the return via the Sierra de Almenara might be challenging for beginners on a racebike. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience and comfort on varied surfaces.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this route in Puerto Lumbreras?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact route are not detailed, Puerto Lumbreras, as a starting point, typically offers public parking options within the town. Look for designated parking zones or street parking near the town center to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region generally benefits from a good climate for cycling outside of the peak summer months. Spring and autumn are ideal, offering pleasant temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning rides essential if cycling during that season.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Sierra de Almenara?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Almenara is a natural area. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads or established tracks within the Sierra de Almenara. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas or private land.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I encounter?
faqs.answer: You'll pass through the mountain pass of Puerto de las Crucecicas and the Sierra de la Almenara. The route also offers a View of Sierra de la Carrasquilla, providing scenic vistas of the surrounding landscapes.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for racebikes and covers public roads and potentially unpaved tracks. While dogs are generally allowed in natural areas, this specific route is not typically considered 'dog-friendly' for accompanying pets due to the nature of road cycling and potential traffic. If you wish to bring a dog, it would need to be in a suitable trailer or carrier, which might impact your cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there places to refill water or find refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Sources of water and refreshments may be scarce, particularly in some parts of the Sierra de Almenara. It is highly recommended to start the route well-stocked with liquids and any necessary snacks. Puerto Lumbreras, your starting point, will have options for stocking up.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other cycling paths or trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the Camino del Alto Guadalentín (20% of the route), Transmurciana 09 (20%), Camino Las Palomas (3%), Vía Verde del FC. Guadix-Almendricos (1%), and Senda de Moisés (1%).

faqs.question: What should I consider bringing for this racebike tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's moderate difficulty and potential for varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your racebike (including spare tubes), and appropriate cycling attire. Sun protection is also crucial, especially during warmer months. Consider tires that can handle some unpaved sections if you plan to stick to the exact return via Sierra de Almenara.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The region of Murcia offers diverse landscapes. You can expect a mix of agricultural lands, inland mountains, and scenic views, particularly from viewpoints like the View of Sierra de la Carrasquilla. The Sierra de Almenara area also features pine forests in some sections, providing welcome shade.

waypoints.name: View of Sierra de la Carrasquilla
